'''Waldan Bridger''' was a bald and burly [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] who served the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] and the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] as a [[Jedi General/Legends|General]] in the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]].

==Biography==
A [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]], Waldan Bridger was [[Jedi training/Legends|educated]] in the ways of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] by the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]], eventually attaining the rank of [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]]. As a Knight, Bridger pursued the path of the [[Jedi Guardian/Legends|Jedi Guardian]] during his independent studies, fashioning for himself a [[San-Ni staff]] which he wielded in place of a [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]]. A ranking [[Jedi Weapon Master]], Bridger eventually was made a [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] by the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi High Council]] for his knowledge and power in the Force.<ref name="TCSWE">''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''</ref>

When the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] erupted between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]], Master Bridger dutifully took up the title of [[Jedi General/Legends|General]] within the newly formed [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]], leading [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]] in battle against the [[Separatist Droid Army/Legends|Separatist Droid Army]]. [[20 BBY/Legends|22 months]] after the start of the war, Bridger led a campaign to bring the war-plagued [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Togoria/Legends|Togoria]] back into the Republic. The [[Togorian/Legends|Togorians]] had voluntarily joined the CIS, however, and viewed the Republic as invaders. When Bridger brought his landing party down on the world, the natives attacked and massacred the clone troopers under Bridger's command. The Togorians were bolstered by the leader of the Confederacy's army, [[General/Legends|General]] [[Grievous/Legends|Grievous]], who saw to it that he alone would face the Jedi. Bridger attacked Grievous head-on, but the duel lasted only twenty [[Standard second|seconds]]. Grievous' lightsabers cut through Bridger's staff and the Jedi Master's body, [[Death/Legends|killing]] him instantly. Grievous, victorious, left Bridger's body where it fell for the ravenous native [[mosgoth]] beasts.<ref name="TCSWE" />

==Behind the scenes==
Waldan Bridger was created by [[Daniel Wallace]] for the canceled guidebook known as ''[[The Essential Guide to Episode I]]''. Along with Jedi [[L'lacielo Sageon]], Bridger was described as serving as one of the Jedi Temple's [[Jedi Temple quartermaster|quartermaster]] at the time of the [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|Invasion of Naboo]]. Both Jedi's first names are anagrams of their creator's name; the letters from both names combined can be reconfigured to spell, 'Daniel Wallace.' The names ''Waldan'' and ''L'lacielo'' are older then the canceled guidebook however. Author [[Abel G. Peña]] originally intended to name one of his own creations "Waldan L'lacielo"; the name was changed during the editing of this particular text to [[Awdrysta Pina]].<ref name=" Endnotes for The Story of General Grievous, Part 2">{{Blog|abelgpena|55/comments|Endnotes for The Story of General Grievous, Part 2: The Knight Slayer}}</ref>
